name: He et al., 'Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition', CVPR 2016

task.description: Create a self-contained 1920x1080 HTML slide titled 'Deep Residual Learning (He et al., 2016)'. Three coexisting, vertically-aligned regions sharing equal panel heights: (A) LEFT (28%): an inline-SVG residual block - input 'x' splits into a path through 'weight layer -> ReLU -> weight layer' and an identity skip-connection arrow that curves around and re-joins at a '+' node, followed by a final 'ReLU'; label the identity arrow 'identity x' and put the formula 'H(x) = F(x) + x' beneath the block. (B) CENTER (38%): a dense paragraph (the provided text) on the degradation problem: 'Deeper plain networks have higher training error than shallower ones - not caused by overfitting but by optimization difficulty. Residual learning reformulates layers to fit a residual mapping F(x) := H(x) - x, which is easier to optimize; if identity is optimal, push residual to zero.' Below it, four bullets: '152 layers, 8x deeper than VGG', 'Lower complexity than VGG-16/19', '3.57% top-5 error on ImageNet (1st, ILSVRC 2015)', 'Won COCO detection & segmentation'. (C) RIGHT (34%): a 6-row ImageNet top-1 error table with header 'Model | top-1 err' and rows 'plain-34 | 28.54', 'ResNet-34 | 25.03', 'ResNet-50 | 22.85', 'ResNet-101 | 21.75', 'ResNet-152 | 21.43'. A footnote pinned to the bottom. ALSO, beside the basic residual block, draw the BOTTLENECK block (1x1 -> 3x3 -> 1x1 conv with identity skip) as a second aligned sub-diagram of equal size sharing the same top and bottom edge as the basic block.
requirements: No element overlaps any other; the skip-connection arrow does not pass through the weight-layer box text
Panels A, B, C share the same top and bottom edge (aligned, equal heights)
The dense degradation paragraph is fully inside panel B with no text clipped or overflowing
The formula 'H(x) = F(x) + x' renders with a real subscript/superscript style (x as identity term) and is fully visible
All five ImageNet table rows plus the header are present with no truncated cells
The four bullets are each at most two lines and fully inside their panel
Nothing overflows the 1920x1080 canvas; no scrollbar appears
The footnote is fully visible above the bottom edge and overlaps nothing
The '+' join node and the identity arrow are both clearly drawn and labeled
Every text element is at least 12px
The basic block and the bottleneck block are drawn as two equal-size sub-diagrams sharing the same top and bottom edge
task.paper: He et al., 'Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition', CVPR 2016

ss_html:
Deep Residual Learning (He et al., 2016)

Basic Residual Block
H(x) = F(x) + x
Bottleneck Block

Deeper plain networks have higher training error than shallower ones - not caused by overfitting but by optimization difficulty. Residual learning reformulates layers to fit a residual mapping F(x) := H(x) - x, which is easier to optimize; if identity is optimal, push residual to zero.

Key Achievements

  • 152 layers, 8× deeper than VGG
  • Lower complexity than VGG-16/19
  • 3.57% top-5 error on ImageNet (1st, ILSVRC 2015)
  • Won COCO detection & segmentation

ImageNet Results

Model Top-1 Error (%)
Plain-34 28.54
ResNet-34 25.03
ResNet-50 22.85
ResNet-101 21.75
ResNet-152 21.43
ResNets revolutionized deep learning by solving the degradation problem through skip connections, enabling training of extremely deep networks with improved performance.
rv_html:
Deep Residual Learning (He et al., 2016)

Basic Residual Block
H(x) = F(x) + x
Bottleneck Block

Deeper plain networks have higher training error than shallower ones - not caused by overfitting but by optimization difficulty. Residual learning reformulates layers to fit a residual mapping F(x) := H(x) - x, which is easier to optimize; if identity is optimal, push residual to zero.

Key Achievements

  • 152 layers, 8× deeper than VGG
  • Lower complexity than VGG-16/19
  • 3.57% top-5 error on ImageNet (1st, ILSVRC 2015)
  • Won COCO detection & segmentation

ImageNet Results

Model Top-1 Error (%)
Plain-34 28.54
ResNet-34 25.03
ResNet-50 22.85
ResNet-101 21.75
ResNet-152 21.43
He, K., Zhang, X., Ren, S., & Sun, J. (2016). Deep residual learning for image recognition. CVPR 2016.

trace.feedback:
Quality score: 85%

Issues found:
  1. [MINOR] The formula 'H(x) = F(x) + x' does not use proper mathematical subscript/superscript styling - the 'x' appears as regular text rather than mathematical notation (at bottom-left)
  2. [MINOR] The skip connection arrows in both the basic block and bottleneck block diagrams appear to pass very close to or potentially through the weight layer boxes, which could affect readability (at top-left)

Suggested fixes:
  1. Apply proper mathematical formatting to the formula with subscripts/superscripts
  2. Adjust the skip connection arrow routing to ensure clear separation from text boxes
  3. Consider slightly increasing the spacing between diagram elements for better visual clarity
